title: Noose hitch
description: A simple wrap-around hitch for tying a rope directly to a tree trunk or thick branch without a carabiner — a common way to rig a hammock or tarp ridgeline. The rope loops around the trunk, then the working end winds around the standing part a few times before being cinched tight. It grips well under load but is still easy to release afterward.
steps: Pass the rope around the trunk or branch.
Cross the working end over the standing part.
Wind the working end around the standing part two or three times.
Pull the wraps snug against the trunk.
